articles.title: Fintech Firm Digital Asset Returns to 4 WTC With 19K-SF Lease
articles.narrative: Fintech firm Digital Asset has signed a 10-year lease for 19,000 square feet at 4 World Trade Center, marking a return to the building it previously occupied. The transaction involved an asking rent of $115 per square foot. The move signifies a strategic decision by the company to re-establish its presence in the Lower Manhattan office tower.

data_points.address: 150 Greenwich Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: 72-story, 2.3 million-square-foot office tower in Lower Manhattan.
data_points.project_notes: Digital Asset previously occupied space in 4 WTC from 2018 until March 2025, when it moved to 107 Greenwich Street.

articles.title: L.A. Closer to Scaling Back ‘Mansion Tax’
articles.narrative: Los Angeles lawmakers are considering a ballot measure to amend Measure ULA, the city's controversial "mansion tax," by exempting newly constructed multifamily properties sold within 10 years of completion. The tax, implemented in April 2023, imposes a 4% to 5.5% levy on property sales above certain thresholds, and while intended for affordable housing and homelessness prevention, it has been criticized for significantly deterring multifamily development. Opponents cite data indicating a substantial drop in high-value real estate deals and new multifamily production, arguing it exacerbates the affordability crisis. Proponents, however, attribute the construction slowdown to broader market conditions and warn that exemptions could reduce funding for housing initiatives.

articles.title: Miami-Area Warehouse Trades for $56M
articles.narrative: Terreno Realty has acquired a 98,000-square-foot industrial distribution building in Hialeah Gardens, FL, for $56.3 million from Clarion Partners. The property, completed in 2022, is fully leased to an e-commerce firm and features high ceilings and extensive loading capabilities. This acquisition expands Terreno Realty's significant industrial portfolio in the Miami market.

data_points.notable_features: The property features 36-foot ceilings, nine dock-high doors, six grade-level loading positions, and parking for 596 cars, located adjacent to the intersection of Florida’s Turnpike and Okeechobee Road.

articles.title: Vilson Lumaj Plans Two 99-Unit Buildings in the Bronx After $14M Deal
articles.narrative: Housing developer Vilson Lumaj has acquired a vacant property in the Bronx for $13.5 million, including air rights, with plans to construct two 99-unit residential buildings. The development strategy of building two separate 99-unit structures is intended to circumvent prevailing wage requirements under the 485-x tax program, a successor to the expired 421-a abatement, which has been criticized for increasing construction costs and hindering housing supply.

data_points.project_notes: The development consists of two 99-unit buildings to avoid prevailing wage requirements under the 485-x tax program. The sale price includes $3.5 million for 60,000 square feet of air rights.

articles.title: Related, Battery Park City Authority to Expand Affordable Housing at Tribeca Park
articles.narrative: Related Companies and the Battery Park City Authority (BPCA) have partnered to preserve and expand affordable housing at Tribeca Park, a 27-story residential building in Lower Manhattan. The agreement amends the building's ground lease, extending the affordability of 81 existing units and adding 20 new units, bringing the total to 101 income-restricted residences through 2069. This initiative is part of BPCA's commitment to increasing affordable housing opportunities in the neighborhood and also includes increased ground rent payments to the authority.

data_points.address: 400 Chambers Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: 27-story residential building near Battery Park.
data_points.project_notes: Agreement amends the building's ground lease to preserve and expand affordable units. Affordability for 101 units (25% of total) is extended through the remainder of the lease term in 2069. Previously, affordability was set to expire in 2029.

articles.title: Real Capital Solutions Acquires Minneapolis Office Property for $34M
articles.narrative: Real Capital Solutions has acquired a 308,681-square-foot office property in Minneapolis for $34 million. The nine-story asset, located at 3701 Wayzata Boulevard, is 99 percent leased and serves as a headquarters location for multiple companies. The property, originally developed for Prudential and later occupied by Target Corp., underwent a significant renovation and repositioning in 2019.

data_points.notable_features: Situated on 25.8 acres overlooking Brownie and Cedar lakes, with amenities including a fitness center, conference facilities, golf simulator, onsite café, outdoor gathering spaces, rooftop patio, bike storage, and direct access to regional trail systems.
data_points.project_notes: Originally developed as a corporate headquarters campus for Prudential and later occupied by Target Corp., the property underwent a comprehensive renovation and repositioning in 2019.

articles.title: CBRE Arranges Sale of Newly Converted 112,862 SF Industrial Building in Northern Indiana
articles.narrative: CBRE has facilitated the sale of a 112,862-square-foot industrial building in Elkhart, Indiana. The property, a former JC Penney store within the redeveloped Concord Mall, was sold by Industrial Commercial Properties to Vault Equity Partners. The building was fully leased to a single tenant at the time of sale, reflecting the successful conversion of retail space into modern industrial use.

data_points.notable_features: Former JC Penney store redeveloped into industrial space with increased clear heights, new loading docks, and drive-in doors.
data_points.project_notes: The seller redeveloped the former Concord Mall into Concord Business Park.

articles.title: Cooper Group Brokers $4.3M Sale of Retail Center in North Olmsted, Ohio
articles.narrative: The Cooper Commercial Investment Group has brokered the sale of Bentley Coe Plaza, a 36,966-square-foot retail center in North Olmsted, Ohio, for $4.3 million. The transaction was completed at a 7.4 percent capitalization rate, with the buyer identified prior to the property being formally marketed for sale.

articles.title: HDA Completes Design of 23,000 SF Nissan Dealership in Herculaneum, Missouri
articles.narrative: HDA has completed the design of the AutoCenters Nissan dealership in Herculaneum, a suburb of St. Louis. The 23,000-square-foot facility includes 8,500 square feet for office, administrative, and customer-facing functions, alongside 14,500 square feet dedicated to service, parts, and detailing.

data_points.notable_features: Features a full curtainwall system for the showroom and a signature red Nissan entry tablet.

articles.title: Nuveen Provides $38.6M in C-PACE Financing for Seniors Housing Development in Central Texas
articles.narrative: Nuveen Green Capital has provided $38.6 million in C-PACE financing for The Statesman at Horseshoe Bay, a new seniors housing community under development in Horseshoe Bay, Texas. The project, developed by Falcon Senior Housing, will offer 180 independent living, assisted living, and memory care units across 313,714 square feet. The financing package also includes EB-5 immigration investment funds.
articles.financing: C-PACE financing and EB-5 immigration investment funds

articles.title: JLL names industry veteran to lead workplace management business

people.name: Chris Fernandez
people.title: Workplace Management (WPM) business lead across the Americas

articles.title: Oxford Partners adds EVP in Houston office
articles.narrative: Oxford Partners has appointed Trey Martin as Executive Vice President to enhance its office and industrial tenant representation services in Houston. Martin brings over two decades of commercial real estate experience, having closed more than $900 million in transactions across various property types, and will focus on expanding the firm's client base and service offerings.

articles.title: Lee & Associates brokers 442,601-square-foot industrial sale in Elgin
articles.narrative: PJP Holdings, LLC, a rigid plastic manufacturing company, acquired a 442,601-square-foot industrial building at 1717 Gifford Drive in Elgin, Illinois, for $24.8 million. This transaction represents the second-largest industrial building sale in the Chicago metropolitan area by square footage in 2026 and the largest in the North Kane / I-90 submarket by both square footage and dollar volume. The buyer plans to modernize the facility and its surrounding infrastructure, expanding its operations in the Chicagoland area.

data_points.notable_features: Second-largest industrial building sale in metropolitan Chicago by square footage in 2026; largest in North Kane / I-90 submarket by square footage and dollar volume.
data_points.project_notes: The sale was influenced by Illinois' 6B tax abatement program.

articles.title: Housing Notes: The spring market was delayed by a month
articles.narrative: The author argues that the spring 2026 housing market experienced a one-month delay due to the Iran War, which began in late February. This conflict abruptly ended a 'Goldilocks' period of stable mortgage rates, pushing them from below 6 percent to around 6.5 percent and shifting market expectations from rate cuts to potential hikes in 2026. Consequently, the recent surge in existing home sales reflects this delayed demand rather than an exceptionally robust spring, suggesting the peak selling season will extend further into the summer. The author also notes that rising energy prices, exacerbated by the war, are contributing to a re-acceleration of inflation, which at 4.2 percent, remains more than double the Fed's 2 percent target.

articles.title: Seattle office building leased to Google hits high-vacancy market
articles.narrative: Vulcan Real Estate has listed the 335,000-square-foot office building at 520 Westlake Avenue North in Seattle's South Lake Union neighborhood for sale. The property, completed in 2021 and leased by a major tech company, also includes ground-floor retail space. The listing comes as Seattle's office market faces high vacancy rates, prompting developers like Vulcan to seek buyers for other large office properties in the Puget Sound region.

data_points.notable_features: Includes 27,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space.
data_points.project_notes: Site was formerly home to a Guitar Center.

articles.market_intelligence: Seattle's office vacancy rate was 30.6 percent in Q1, higher than San Francisco and Los Angeles. Office absorption in Seattle was negative in Q1, with a 412,000-square-foot gap between space vacated and leased.

articles.title: The Data Drop: GSA’s rent bill vs. DOGE
articles.narrative: This article, a weekly digest of real estate data, covers several key trends. It revisits the federal government's efforts to cut its rent bill, finding minimal change despite initial ambitions. The report also highlights a national increase in underwater homes, reaching over 2 million for the first time since 2021, driven by slowing home price growth and high borrowing costs. In Chicago, residential permit approvals are on the rise, signaling more housing units, even as the city faces a high number of foreclosure filings. The article also notes the escalating cost of the Obama Presidential Center and details top commercial and residential sales in New York City.

data_points.address: 7 Dey Street, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: SL Green Realty will retain the office space in the building.

articles.title: The Daily Dirt: A series of mysterious sales
articles.narrative: This article investigates a series of three rapid sales of a 29-unit rent-stabilized walk-up building in East Harlem, New York City, between February 2025 and January 2026, with prices escalating from $285,110 to $1.636 million, and finally to $3 million. The initial low price was attributed to a mezzanine lender forcing a sale from a previous owner who had overpaid for a portfolio of rent-stabilized buildings before the 2019 Housing Stability and Tenant Protection Act. The author explores the significant price discrepancies and the parties involved in each transaction, noting that the rent stabilization laws likely prevented major renovations that could justify such price increases, raising questions about the dynamics of rent-stabilized property sales.

articles.title: NYC’s top deals: Williamsburg commercial properties sell for $50M
articles.narrative: Norworth Holdings LLC acquired a portfolio of commercial properties in Williamsburg, Brooklyn, for $50 million. The portfolio includes a nearly 38,000-square-foot commercial condo, a nearly 70,000-square-foot warehouse, and a vacant lot, collectively occupying most of a city block. The sellers were companies managed by Abraham Follman.

data_points.address: 18 Warsoff Place, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: The sale included a commercial condo at 18 Warsoff Place, a warehouse at 39 Walworth Street, and a vacant lot.

articles.title: How an alleged California fraudster seized a regional bank and swindled a seasoned landlord
articles.narrative: Federal agents have arrested Mahender Makhijani for allegedly defrauding Western Alliance Bancorp out of $100 million through a scheme involving real estate plays and manipulated title policies. Veteran landlord Gerald Marcil, who owns thousands of multifamily units in Southern California, claims he was duped by Makhijani into guaranteeing loans and signing documents without full disclosure, leading to significant debt and legal bills. Marcil has filed two lawsuits alleging fraud and elder abuse, detailing how Makhijani secretly placed junior liens on properties, pocketed sale proceeds, and diverted funds from capital calls and rental income. The alleged scheme also involved Makhijani's control over Nano Banc, which Marcil claims was used to manipulate him into signing a $19.2 million loan and an indemnity agreement, ultimately threatening foreclosure on his portfolio.

articles.title: Free at last? Charles Cohen pays off $187M Fortress debt
articles.narrative: Billionaire Charles Cohen has satisfied a debt of over $187 million to Fortress Investment Group, stemming from personal guarantees on a defaulted $534 million loan portfolio. This payment concludes a significant legal battle that began in 2022 and led to Cohen selling several Manhattan properties to generate funds. Despite this resolution, Fortress indicates it will continue to pursue other claims against Cohen.

data_points.project_notes: The debt payment resolves a judgment against Charles Cohen related to guarantees on a $534 million loan portfolio that defaulted in 2024. Cohen sold 623 Fifth Avenue and 3 East 54th Street to Vornado Realty Trust for $218 million and $141 million respectively, and the ground beneath 111 West 33rd Street and 1400 Broadway to Empire State Realty Trust for $110 million to help satisfy the debt. Cohen also lost 750 Lexington Avenue at a foreclosure auction earlier this year.

articles.title: Terra, BH to pay $100M for billionaire Jeff Greene’s West Palm land
articles.narrative: Terra and BH Group are set to acquire a 1.6-acre waterfront site in West Palm Beach from billionaire Jeff Greene for approximately $100 million. The developers plan to construct two 31-story condominium towers on the property, which will include 281 residential units, retail space, and a shared underground parking garage. The site's value has seen a significant increase since its last sale in 2014, reflecting strong demand for prime waterfront development opportunities in the area.

data_points.address: 2085 North Flagler Drive, West Palm Beach, FL
data_points.notable_features: Waterfront site near Northwood Village and across from the renovated Currie Park. The development will include third-floor amenity space, a ninth-floor pool, a rooftop amenity area on one tower, and a penthouse atop the other tower.
data_points.project_notes: The land was previously acquired by the seller in 2014 for $1.5 million.

articles.market_intelligence: More than 2,000 condos are currently in development in West Palm Beach.

articles.title: Billionaire video game developer pays $71M for McMackins’ waterfront Manalapan estate
articles.narrative: Billionaire video game developer Gabe Newell, through Building Florida Home LLC, acquired a 22,900-square-foot waterfront estate in Manalapan, FL, from Cindy and Ron McMackin for $70.9 million. The transaction was financed with a $46 million mortgage from Morgan Stanley. The property, built in 2020, features a unique tunnel connecting the mansion to a beach bungalow and was sold for approximately 17 percent below its most recent asking price.

data_points.notable_features: Waterfront estate with a tunnel connecting the mansion to an air-conditioned beach bungalow.
data_points.project_notes: The sellers previously acquired the property for $38.9 million in 2020.

articles.title: Costco, multifamily on tap as mixed-use projects power Pompano Beach
articles.narrative: Pompano Beach is experiencing a surge in mixed-use development, with two significant projects heading to the city's planning and zoning board for review. One proposal involves a new 163,000-square-foot Costco Wholesale store, complete with a tire center and gas station, to be built on an 18.7-acre parcel within The Pomp master-planned development. The other project, by Vera Fund, is a 132-unit multifamily building at 1600 South Federal Highway, which will also include 3,700 square feet of retail space and retain an existing office building on the site. These developments reflect Pompano Beach's growing appeal to developers seeking opportunities outside of more established South Florida markets.
articles.market_intelligence: South Florida is a leader in mixed-use real estate due to high population growth, zoning flexibility, investor-friendly policies, and demand for lifestyle integration.

articles.title: South Florida by the numbers: The World Cup Effect
articles.narrative: The FIFA World Cup is expected to bring between 600,000 and 1 million visitors to South Florida, generating an estimated financial impact of $1.3 billion for the region. This influx of international attention is anticipated to significantly boost the local real estate market, with developers actively targeting buyers from Latin American countries. The tournament is also projected to create 9,000 local jobs and lead to a 200 percent increase in Broward County hotel room rates during peak periods, demonstrating a substantial economic uplift across various sectors.

articles.title: Developer Michael Klinger sells waterfront Bal Harbour mansion for $43M
articles.narrative: Developer Michael Klinger, through his firm Saber South Real Estate, has sold a waterfront mansion at 56 Bal Bay Drive in Bal Harbour, FL for nearly $43 million. This transaction marks the highest-priced home sale in the village this year, significantly exceeding Klinger's initial $30 million sales expectation after an extensive renovation of the property, which he acquired in 2022 for $15.4 million. The sale highlights the continued strength and appreciation in South Florida's luxury residential market, attracting buyers from high-tax states.

data_points.address: 56 Bal Bay Drive, Bal Harbour, FL
data_points.notable_features: Modern home designed by NMD Nomadas with seven bedrooms and eight and a half bathrooms, featuring 100 feet of Intracoastal Waterway frontage.
data_points.project_notes: The property was acquired by Klinger's entities in 2022 for $15.4 million, with plans for extensive renovation. Klinger kept the shell of the original 2008-built house.

articles.market_intelligence: The luxury residential market in South Florida continues to see rising prices and record-setting trades, even during traditionally slower summer months, with significant buyer interest from California and other high-tax states.

articles.title: Gauging the Obama Center’s real estate ripple effects across the South Side
articles.narrative: The Obama Presidential Center, a 19-acre development on Chicago's South Side, has seen its construction costs escalate to $850 million. While intended as an economic catalyst, the project has fueled real estate speculation, leading to a doubling of home prices in East Woodlawn between 2019 and 2025. This surge has attracted out-of-state investors to the South Shore multifamily market, driving up unit costs but also resulting in a wave of foreclosures and property mismanagement due to a disconnect from local renter incomes. The situation has prompted legislative efforts to protect tenant rights and preserve affordability.

data_points.address: 6001 S Stony Island Ave, Chicago, IL
data_points.notable_features: Features a 225-foot museum building wrapped in concrete letters from a historic Selma speech.
data_points.project_notes: Construction costs ballooned from an initial $300 million estimate; faced a half-decade legal battle and pandemic-related construction delays.

articles.market_intelligence: The Institute for Housing Studies at DePaul University reported that single-family and multifamily home prices in East Woodlawn doubled between 2019 and 2025, reaching a median of $440,000, significantly outpacing the rate of home price growth for Illinois. The Cook County Assessor’s Office data, cited by Crain’s, indicates that the inventory of vacant lots in the 60637 ZIP code decreased from 964 to 798 over ten years. DePaul University also found that the average cost of lower-end multifamily units in South Shore increased from less than $60,000 in 2019 to $73,000 per unit in 2024. The Real Deal reported that in 2024, lenders filed 20 foreclosure lawsuits against South Shore buildings with five or more units, the highest volume in a decade for any Chicago neighborhood, and nearly a quarter of all delinquent Chicago multifamily loans in the CMBS market originate from South Shore.

articles.title: Chicago’s top construction permits: Week ending June 16, 2026
articles.narrative: This article summarizes the top construction permits issued in Chicago for the week ending June 16, 2026, highlighting several significant alteration projects. The largest permit was for elevator installations at the Thompson Center, a joint venture between The Prime Group and Capri Investment Group, with Google also securing an electrical permit for the building. Other notable projects include a commercial renovation at 10 South Riverside Plaza, an interior renovation at Washington Irving Elementary School, a tenant improvement for Amwins Insurance Brokerage at 10 South La Salle Street, a plumbing infrastructure upgrade at a condo building at 5801 North Sheridan Road, and a data center upgrade for Netratlity Data Centers at 717 South Wells Street.

articles.title: Lake Geneva’s historic Allegheny estate lists for $24M as priciest in southern Wisconsin
articles.narrative: The historic Allegheny estate in Lake Geneva, Wisconsin, has been listed for $24 million, making it the most expensive active listing in southern Wisconsin. The nearly 21-acre waterfront property features a 102-year-old Colonial-style mansion and 560 feet of Geneva Lake shoreline. The estate, designed by architect Howard Van Doren Shaw, includes original architectural details and is protected by a conservation easement preventing subdivision. A sale at this price would be one of only a few transactions in the area to exceed $20 million.

data_points.address: 3150 S Lake Shore Dr, Lake Geneva, WI
data_points.notable_features: Features 560 feet of Geneva Lake shoreline and a 102-year-old Colonial-style mansion designed by Howard Van Doren Shaw.
data_points.project_notes: The estate's acreage is under a conservation easement, prohibiting subdivision for new home development. The home was completed in 1925 for industrialist Lewis Myers.

articles.title: Oakley Home Builders snags Burr Ridge equestrian estate for well below $10M ask
articles.narrative: Oakley Home Builders acquired Saddle Hill Farm, a 7.6-acre equestrian estate in Burr Ridge, IL, for $6 million, representing a 40 percent discount from its original $10 million asking price. The developer plans to demolish the existing structures and construct a 16-home active-adult subdivision for buyers aged 55 and older. The project will feature 3,700-square-foot homes with first-floor primary suites and will include 1.5 acres of green space, a pond, pocket parks, and a walking path, pending a necessary zoning change.

data_points.address: 6191 S County Line Rd, Burr Ridge, IL
data_points.notable_features: Proposed 16-home active-adult community for buyers aged 55 and older, with 3,700-square-foot homes featuring first-floor primary suites, step-free access, and walk-in showers.
data_points.project_notes: The 2002 purchase of the original four-acre farm was for $1.65 million, with adjacent acreage added in 2007. The developer plans to demolish the 1940s-era main house, barn, and paddocks. A zoning change is required to build 16 homes, as current zoning caps density at eight.

articles.market_intelligence: The $6 million closing is the second-priciest Burr Ridge transaction in five years, following a $6.9 million sale in 2022. Developer appetite for multi-acre estates in Burr Ridge has increased, with another 4.7-acre property on Madison Street selling for $3.5 million in February.

articles.title: Local firms snag distressed Dallas-Fort Worth apartments after foreclosure sales
articles.narrative: Two distressed multifamily properties in North Texas, the 152-unit Apex Apartments in Fort Worth and the 149-unit Residences at White Rock Lake in Dallas, were recently sold after undergoing foreclosure auctions. The Residences at White Rock Lake was acquired by an entity linked to Ironridge Capital, while Apex Apartments was purchased by Ninety9 Capital. Both transactions involved the disposition of properties by entities that had previously acquired them through foreclosure.

data_points.notable_features: Located off North Buckner Boulevard, southeast of White Rock Lake.
data_points.project_notes: Previously acquired by Trevian Capital at a foreclosure auction for $8.5 million in February 2026.

companies_people.label: BUYER
companies_people.firm_name: Residences at White Rock Lake LLC

articles.title: Celestica doubles down on Richardson with lease renewal, planned expansion
articles.narrative: Celestica, a data center infrastructure company, has renewed its 10-year lease for its 600,000-square-foot manufacturing facility at 2801 Telecom Parkway in Richardson, TX. The company also plans a significant expansion into an additional 343,000 square feet of office space by next year. This move is supported by a $3 million tenant improvement grant from the City of Richardson, which anticipates the retention of 400 jobs and the creation of 2,300 new permanent jobs, alongside a $300 million capital investment from Celestica.

data_points.notable_features: Manufacturing hub with planned office expansion, located in the Telecom Parkway corridor.
data_points.project_notes: City of Richardson approved a $3 million tenant improvement grant. Celestica plans to spend $300 million in capital investments over the next two years.

articles.title: Universal Music plans first US branded condo project at contentious Hill Country site
articles.narrative: Universal Music Group is partnering with Austin-based White Rocks Entertainment to develop UMusic Hotel & Private Residences Austin, a branded condo and hotel project in the Hill Country near Bee Cave. The development will feature 600 condo units, 150 hotel rooms, and a 10,000-seat outdoor venue, with construction anticipated to begin in early 2027 and conclude in 2030, pending regulatory approvals. This marks Universal Music's first hospitality project in the United States, with condo pricing expected to start at $2.3 million.

data_points.notable_features: Proposed amenities include professional recording studios, a nightclub, comedy club, indoor performance venue, and two restaurants. The project is designed to meet a 100 percent water reuse standard, with over 70 percent of the site dedicated to open space.
data_points.project_notes: The project is currently awaiting approval from the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality for its wastewater permit. A similar project, Violet Crown, was previously proposed for the site in 2021 but was withdrawn due to local opposition over environmental concerns.

articles.title: Trademark lassos Whole Foods for Southlake mixed-use development
articles.narrative: Trademark Property Company has officially secured Whole Foods Market as the anchor tenant for its Shivers Farm mixed-use development in Southlake, TX. The 40-acre project, approved by the Southlake City Council, will feature retail, office space, single-family lots, and a hotel or additional retail pad. Construction is slated to begin this summer and conclude by the end of 2027, bringing the first major grocer north of State Highway 114 to the area.

data_points.address: 1900 N White Chapel Blvd, Southlake, TX
data_points.notable_features: Includes 111,000 SF of retail, 38,000 SF of office, 37 single-family lots, a three-acre pad for hotel/retail, and a one-acre park. It will be the first major grocer north of State Highway 114.
data_points.project_notes: The site was previously owned by the Shivers family since the 19th century. Southlake City Council approved the project's master development agreement.

articles.market_intelligence: North Texas leads the nation in retail construction, with Dallas having a pipeline of nearly 7 million square feet of retail real estate, almost 5 million square feet of which is pre-leased.

articles.title: Toyota’s $2B San Antonio expansion hangs on incentive vote
articles.narrative: San Antonio city leaders are optimistic about securing a proposed $2 billion manufacturing expansion by Toyota, which would double the size of the automaker's South Side campus and create approximately 2,000 jobs. The City Council is preparing to vote on an economic incentives package to support "Project Orca," which would be one of the largest industrial investments in the city in recent years. This expansion would further solidify Toyota's two-decade presence in the region and enhance San Antonio's reputation as a manufacturing hub with strong cross-border supply chain access.

data_points.notable_features: Proposed expansion would roughly double the size of the existing campus and create approximately 2,000 jobs.
data_points.project_notes: The project is circulating under the code name "Project Orca." Toyota is also planning a new 32,000-square-foot office project at the campus, representing an additional $18.2 million investment.

articles.market_intelligence: Interest from major employers remains strong in markets with available industrial land and workforce capacity. San Antonio's proximity to northern Mexico, extensive interstate connectivity, and growing advanced manufacturing workforce are key selling points for corporate prospects.

articles.title: SF landlord accused of killing tenant to clear Sunset home for sale
articles.narrative: A San Francisco landlord is facing a wrongful death lawsuit alleging he murdered his tenant to clear a Sunset District home for sale. The suit claims the landlord engaged in a months-long campaign to force the tenant out, including issuing improper notices, threatening eviction, and refusing rent payments, culminating in the tenant's fatal shooting. The landlord has been arrested and charged with murder, arson, and weapons offenses.

data_points.project_notes: Tenant had rented the single-family home since 2023, paying $3,200 per month in cash.

articles.title: SummerHill, Cypress plot San Jose office-to-resi redev with affordable apartments
articles.narrative: SummerHill Homes and Cypress Equity Investments are under contract to acquire a 5.9-acre site in San Jose from LBA Realty, with plans to redevelop a vacant office building into a mixed-income residential community. The project will feature 373 affordable apartments and 73 market-rate townhouse-style condominiums, addressing the growing demand for housing in Silicon Valley driven by the influx of tech and AI workers. The affordable units will target households earning 60% of the area median income.

data_points.address: 3550 North First Street, San Jose, CA
data_points.notable_features: Redevelopment of a vacant office building into a mixed-income residential community.
data_points.project_notes: The 100% affordable apartment component will have an affordability target of 60% of the area median income. The site was previously acquired by an LBA affiliate in February 2025 for $18.5 million.

articles.market_intelligence: The Santa Clara County area median income is $143,850 for one person or $205,500 for a household of four. 60% of the area median income works out to roughly $86,310 for an individual renter or $123,300 for a four-person home. The site's assessed value was $32.4 million in January 2025.

articles.title: Can Brownfield Sites Support Data Centers?
articles.narrative: This article explores the potential for brownfield sites to host data centers, highlighting both the advantages and challenges. While brownfields offer benefits like proximity to power grids, favorable zoning, and existing infrastructure, they also present significant environmental liabilities. The authors, legal experts from Bracewell LLP, emphasize the need for thorough due diligence, clear allocation of environmental risks in contracts, and consideration of voluntary cleanup programs. They also touch upon policy efforts to streamline environmental permits for data center development, suggesting that developers should monitor new incentives while utilizing existing strategies for brownfield redevelopment.

articles.title: The Human Side of Automation in Multifamily Property Management
articles.narrative: This article explores the evolving role of automation and artificial intelligence in multifamily property management, emphasizing that technology serves as a tool to enhance efficiency rather than replace human staff. Industry leaders highlight that automation frees up onsite teams from administrative tasks, allowing them to focus on creating positive resident experiences, building relationships, and addressing higher-value operational needs. While AI streamlines tasks like lead nurturing, tour scheduling, and data analysis, human professionals remain crucial for interpreting data, making strategic decisions, and fostering essential resident relationships that drive satisfaction and retention.

articles.title: Housing Affordability Through RVs and Travel Trailers
articles.narrative: This article explores the concept of recreational vehicles (RVs), travel trailers, and tiny homes as a potential solution to the housing affordability crisis. It highlights the increasing number of people living in RVs full-time, often due to economic necessity, but notes that current U.S. Building Codes classify RVs as vehicles, not year-round housing. The piece discusses existing RV communities in states with more lenient zoning and advocates for regulatory changes, such as removing the "no year-round" sticker on RVs and updating building codes to create standalone standards for tiny homes, to facilitate their use as permanent dwellings.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

articles.title: Survey Reports Robust Leasing and Design Investments in the Legal Office Sector
articles.narrative: The legal sector is demonstrating robust performance in the office market, driven by strong demand for services, revenue growth, and a commitment to in-person work. A recent survey by Cushman & Wakefield indicates that 4.5 million square feet of legal office space was leased in Q1 2026, with firms increasingly expanding and facing higher renewal rates due to tight supply. Law firms are prioritizing office design that emphasizes employee well-being, collaborative spaces, and areas for focused work, while also integrating technology like artificial intelligence for document generation and analytics, despite concerns about AI's accuracy. The sector anticipates continued employment growth and geographic expansion, with successful firms expected to align their real estate, workforce, and technology strategies.

articles.title: Colliers Facilitates Sale of 118-Unit Apartment Property in Bozeman
articles.narrative: Colliers facilitated the sale of Silver Creek Apartments, a 118-unit multifamily community in Bozeman, Montana. The property, built in 2021, benefits from its strategic location near Downtown Bozeman, Montana State University, and key employment and retail corridors, which are expected to drive strong, sustainable demand and long-term value in the growing market.

data_points.notable_features: Located near Downtown Bozeman, Montana State University, and major employment and retail corridors.

articles.title: ICE Looks To Offload $700M In U.S. Warehouses As Detention Overhaul Unravels
articles.narrative: The Department of Homeland Security is reversing its controversial plan to convert industrial warehouses into detention facilities, now seeking to sell or transfer seven of the 11 properties it acquired for over $700 million. This marks a significant shift from the agency's initial $38.3 billion expansion strategy, which aimed to increase detention capacity nationwide. The decision follows a halt to the program and an inspector general probe into its implementation, as well as legal challenges in several states regarding environmental reviews and property conversions. The properties, which were purchased at a premium, now face an uncertain return to the market, potentially at a discount due to their association with the federal government's contested real estate initiative.

data_points.project_notes: The Department of Homeland Security is looking to offload seven out of 11 facilities it acquired for over $700 million, reversing a plan to convert them into detention centers. The agency plans to move forward with four facilities and intends to buy facilities from private prison companies. The properties were purchased at 11% to 13% above market value.

articles.title: CRE Pricing Spread Between Cycle's 'Winners And Laggards' The Widest Ever
articles.narrative: A recent report from Altus Group highlights a record-setting divergence in commercial real estate pricing between top-performing and struggling asset classes. Industrial property pricing has surged 88.5% since pre-pandemic levels, while office pricing has only increased by 36.6% over the same period, indicating a structural repricing rather than a mere recovery gap. Despite anticipated headwinds like elevated interest rates, geopolitical tensions, and tech sector volatility, overall CRE prices saw gains in Q1 2026, with all property types except hospitality reaching new highs. The report notes that industrial remains the most affordable on a per-square-foot basis at $110, while multifamily is the most expensive at $150.

articles.title: 20 Attorneys General Sue To Block Executive Order Targeting DEI In Federal Contracts
articles.narrative: Attorneys general from 19 states and the District of Columbia have filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration, seeking to block an executive order that targets diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) efforts among federal contractors. The lawsuit argues that the executive order, titled "Addressing DEI Discrimination by Federal Contractors," and its resulting rules are arbitrary, capricious, and unlawfully implemented without proper public comment periods. The states contend that the new rules, which require contractors to avoid "racially discriminatory DEI activities" and make internal records available for compliance, are vague, increase compliance costs, and hinder economic growth, potentially leading to contract termination or exclusion from government leasing opportunities for non-compliance. The legal challenge highlights the significant impact on federal contracts, including construction work and all General Services Administration leases, and seeks an injunction to prevent the order's implementation.
